title: leptospermum coriaceum
intro: Leptospermum coriaceum, commonly known as the Tasmanian blue gum, is a species of flowering plant in the myrtle family, Myrtaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Leptospermum coriaceum include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
